topics.title: Past Simple — Regular Verbs
topics.explanation: For things that already happened, add "-ed" to regular verbs: play → played, watch → watched.
topics.explanationAr: للأشياء التي حدثت بالفعل، نضيف "-ed" للأفعال المنتظمة.

examples.en: I played football yesterday.
She watched a movie last night.
We visited my aunt last weekend.
examples.ar: زرنا عمتي في نهاية الأسبوع الماضي.

examples.en: They didn't finish their homework.

topics.title: Past Simple — Irregular Verbs
topics.explanation: Some verbs don't follow the "-ed" rule and change completely: go → went, eat → ate, see → saw.
topics.explanationAr: بعض الأفعال لا تتبع قاعدة "-ed" وتتغير تمامًا: go → went.

examples.en: We went to the beach last summer.
I ate breakfast at seven.
She saw a beautiful bird in the park.
He came home late yesterday.

topics.title: Past Simple Negatives & Questions
topics.explanation: We use "didn't" for negatives and "did" for questions, and the main verb goes back to its base form.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "didn't" للنفي و"did" للسؤال، ويعود الفعل الرئيسي إلى صيغته الأساسية.

examples.en: I didn't watch TV last night.
Did you finish your homework?
Where did you go last weekend?
examples.ar: أين ذهبت في نهاية الأسبوع الماضي؟

examples.en: She didn't like the movie.

topics.title: Was / Were
topics.explanation: "Was" is the past form of "is/am" (I was, he/she/it was). "Were" is the past form of "are" (you/we/they were).
topics.explanationAr: "was" هي الماضي من "is/am"، و"were" هي الماضي من "are".

examples.en: I was at school yesterday.
They were happy about the trip.
Was she at the party?
We weren't ready for the test.

topics.title: There was / There were
topics.explanation: The past form of "there is/are". Use "there was" for one thing, "there were" for more than one.
topics.explanationAr: صيغة الماضي من "there is/are". نستخدم "there was" لشيء واحد و"there were" لأكثر من شيء.

examples.en: There was a big cake on the table.
examples.ar: كانت توجد كعكة كبيرة على الطاولة.

examples.en: There were many students at the event.
examples.ar: كان يوجد الكثير من الطلاب في الفعالية.

examples.en: There wasn't any food left.
Were there any tickets available?

topics.title: Countable & Uncountable Nouns
topics.explanation: Countable nouns can be counted (one apple, two apples). Uncountable nouns cannot (water, rice, information) and have no plural form.
topics.explanationAr: الأسماء المعدودة يمكن عدّها، أما غير المعدودة فلا يمكن عدّها وليس لها صيغة جمع.

examples.en: I have three books on my desk.
Can I have some water, please?
examples.ar: هل يمكنني الحصول على بعض الماء من فضلك؟

examples.en: She gave me some good advice.
There is a lot of rice in the bowl.
examples.ar: يوجد الكثير من الأرز في الوعاء.

topics.title: How much / How many
topics.explanation: Use "how many" with countable nouns and "how much" with uncountable nouns to ask about quantity.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "how many" مع الأسماء المعدودة و"how much" مع غير المعدودة للسؤال عن الكمية.

examples.en: How many students are in your class?
How much water do you drink every day?
examples.ar: كم كمية الماء التي تشربها كل يوم؟

examples.en: How many apples do we need?
How much money do you have?

topics.title: Time Expressions (ago, last, yesterday)
topics.explanation: We use words like "ago", "last", and "yesterday" to talk about when something happened in the past.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم كلمات مثل "ago" و"last" و"yesterday" للحديث عن وقت حدوث شيء في الماضي.

examples.en: I visited Cairo two years ago.
We went swimming last summer.
I saw her yesterday at school.
He called me last night.
